question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user upload crafted signature files to the public cloud bucket (S3/GCP) with all-null `fileHashSignature` fields so that every call to `verifySignature()` returns false, causing `consensusValidator.validate()` to throw `SignatureVerificationException` for every stream file and permanently halting transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user replace legitimate node signature files in the publicly-writable cloud bucket with files whose `fileHashSignature` bytes are all zeros, causing `sig.verify()` to return false for every node, preventing `SignatureStatus.VERIFIED` from ever being set and blocking all consensus, resulting in a total network shutdown of the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an empty `Collection<StreamFileSignature>` to `verify()` so that the for-loop over signatures is skipped entirely, `consensusValidator.validate()` receives an empty collection, and the `signatureHashMap` remains empty, causing `consensusCount` to stay zero and throwing `SignatureVerificationException`, effectively shutting down transaction processing?
